transcript: Speaker A: काय रे? function मध्ये एकदम गप्प बसला होता. Promotion ची news सांगायची होती ना तुला? [What's up? You were totally quiet at the function. Weren't you supposed to share your promotion news?] Speaker B: सांगायला सुरुवात केली होती. पण NRI cousin English मध्ये बोलायला लागला. Topic बदलला आणि माझा moment गेला. [I had started to say it. But the NRI cousin started speaking in English. The topic changed and my moment was gone.] Speaker A: Exactly काय झालं? [Exactly what happened?] Speaker B: NRI cousin fast English मध्ये बोलत होता. "Actually I got a promotion last month" मला ते already पुढे गेले होते. आपल्याच घरी मला सांगता नाही आलं सर्व. [The NRI cousin was speaking fast English. "Actually I got a promotion last month" - by the time I could say this, they had already moved on. I couldn't even share it in my own home.] Speaker A: ते सर्व तुला ऐकायचं होतं ना? Important news होती. [You wanted them to hear all that, right? It was important news.] Speaker B: माहिती आहे. पण तेव्हा शब्दच आले नाही. आपल्याच घरी परका वाटायचा. [I know. But the words just didn't come out then. Felt like an outsider in my own home.] Speaker A: SpeakX use करतो मी. Real conversation practice. Family settings, social situations, सगळं. Language switch smoothly व्हायला लागते. Try कर एकदा. [I use SpeakX. Real conversation practice. Family settings, social situations, everything. Language switching becomes smooth. Try it once.] Speaker B: आणि परकं वाटणं बंद होईल? [And will I stop feeling like an outsider?] Speaker A: आपलीच family आहे. तुझ्या शब्दात बोल. [It's our own family. Speak in your own words.] Speaker A: मग आज कोण बोलत होतं सगळ्यांना? [So, who was speaking to everyone today?]
on_screen_text: function madhe ekdum gapp hota [Was totally quiet in the function]; sangaychee hoti na tula? [Wanted to tell you, right?]; Sangayla suruvaat keli hoti [Had started to tell]; pan NRI cousin English [but NRI cousin English]; madhe bolayala laagla [started speaking in]; topic badalla [topic changed]; aani maza moment gela [and my moment was gone]; Exactly kay zala? [Exactly what happened?]; NRI cousin fast English [NRI cousin fast English]; madhe bolat hota [was speaking in]; actually i got a [actually i got a]; promotion last month [promotion last month]; mala te already pudhe gele hote [they had already moved ahead]; apne ghari mala shata [couldn't tell at my own home]; te sab tula aikaycha hote na [you wanted them to hear all that, right?]; important news hoti [it was important news]; Maahit aahe [I know]; Pan tevha shabdaat chale [But then words didn't come]; Aaplyach ghari paraka wataycha [Felt like an outsider in my own home]; Language switch [Language switch]; smoothly vhayla lagte [starts happening smoothly]; Aani swatahcha watna band hoil? [And will I stop feeling like an outsider?]; Aapla family aahe [It's our family]; tuzya shabdaat bol [speak in your own words]; Mag aaj kaun bolta hota saglyana? [So who was speaking to everyone today?]; Family shouldn't feel foreign; Speak your world; SpeakX; Download Now
summary: A young man feels left out and unable to share his promotion news at a family gathering because his NRI cousin speaks fast English. His friend recommends the SpeakX app for real-life conversation practice to build confidence. The ad ends with the user successfully speaking English and feeling included, prompting viewers to download the app.
